disclaimer: Community-maintained planning heuristic; verify against your current in-game tooltips and character sheet.

classes.name: Barbarian
classes.summary: The barbarian is a brutal warrior, often hailing from a tribal background, with elite barbarians often linked by ancestry to the Vrykul of Northrend. Generally simple of mind and with no magic of their own, barbarians rely on their weapons to earn their place in the world.

specs.name: Brutality
specs.description: The core offensive tree — raw strength, fury and execute power.
specs.playstyle: The core Barbarian damage tree: build Carnage stacks on enemies with relentless Physical attacks, then consume them for Strength-scaled burst. You set up your kill windows in advance — stack, then decapitate. Almost pure Physical damage; simple, fast and forgiving.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Armor Penetration > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Agility > Hit (to cap) > Critical Strike > Expertise > Haste
statPriority.note: Pure Physical builder-spender, but Agility (not Strength) is the primary attribute: Ancestral Might turns Agility straight into attack power and Body Builder converts 3 Agility into 1 Strength (which Ogre's Strength then re-converts to AP), so Agility scales AP through two paths while Strength only helps as an Agi sink. After the Hit/Expertise caps, stack Agility, then Crit (Brutal Strikes amplifies physical crit damage and Battle Rhythm/Blood Pact chain off critical strikes) over Armor Pen and Haste. Live-server note: top players also rate Armor Penetration highly (some above Crit) and stack Strength through external buffs for its large AP conversion — chase group Strength/attribute buffs, and many run leather for the baseline Agility.

weapon.note: Plays naturally dual-wield — Smash and Barbaric Whirl both hit "with both weapons" and a competitive 1H build exists — but a two-hander also works (Battering Ram rewards a 2H). Run whichever your gear favors.

specs.name: Headhunting
specs.description: Berserker rushes, impaling spears and bleed/puncture pressure.
specs.playstyle: A spear-thrower's tree layered on the melee core: Impaling Spear and Berserker Rush let you open at range, close hard, and finish in melee. Plays as momentum DPS — you commit to a target and ride the rush window.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Armor Penetration > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Agility > Hit (to cap) > Critical Strike > Expertise > Haste
statPriority.note: Thrown/burst physical spec whose primary stat is Agility, not Strength: Power Tosser makes Throw Weapon scale off Agility and the shared Ancestral Might (Agi->AP) plus Body Builder (Agi->Str->AP) conversions make Agility the top scaler. Land the opener (Hit/Expertise) first, then Agility, then Crit (Frothing and the crit-payoff finishers reward it) over Armor Pen and Haste.

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

specs.name: Ancestry
specs.description: Vrykul/frost-themed shouts, totems and party support.
specs.playstyle: Supportive shouts and Vrykul ancestor magic: Frost Giant themes, Clanlord's Totem, rally effects and morale debuffs. You keep group-wide buffs rolling ahead of the fight rather than reacting to it, with enough toughness to off-tank in a pinch.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Agility > Haste > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Spirit
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Agility > Hit (to cap) > Haste
statPriority.note: Sustained physical support spec built on Agility, not Strength: Ancestral Might converts Agility to attack power, Deadly Duo scales off Agility, and Body Builder turns Agility into Strength - so Agility is the primary attribute. Still pure physical (Splash Zone's spell-power line is a Spirit-scaled ally buff, not the Barbarian casting). After Hit and Agility, Haste is valued to refresh procs/totem uptime, with a touch of Spirit for Splash Zone magnitude and Ancestral Roar self-healing.

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Thane's Guard requires a shield.

classes.name: Witch Doctor
classes.summary: The Witch Doctor is an ancient, deep-seated aspect of troll culture in Azeroth, using terrible voodoo magic, wards and potions in their vile rituals. A Horde-only class, the Darkspear trolls have recently shared their voodoo tradition with the Orcs of the Horde to better combat the Alliance.

specs.name: Voodoo
specs.description: Curse your enemies with shadowy hexes and invoke the dark Loa Spirits.
specs.playstyle: Curse-slinging shadow caster: hexes, Voodoo zones and dark Loa invocations. Heavy crowd-control signature (5 CC effects in the kit) — you shape the fight with hexes and zones laid down early, then let the curses do the work. Stack a Loa's buff to 5 for the strong payoff.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spirit > Spell Power > Haste > Critical Strike > Intellect
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spirit > Spell Hit (cap) > Spell Power > Haste > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: This is a Spirit-scaling caster, not a plain Intellect/SP caster. Voodoo Power converts Spirit into both spell damage AND spell hit rating, and the core nukes carry huge flat Spirit coefficients (Bad Juju Spirit, Hexfire Spirit); Staff of the Coven and The Price of Power stack more Spirit%. So Spirit is the top scaling attribute (it even helps reach the hit cap) and belongs near the front, not buried behind Intellect/MP5. Intellect takes the caster badge only because Spirit isn't a badge option. Haste still valued for extra DoT/hex ticks (Quickened Curses, Juju, Alchemical Enhancement), Crit behind it via De Other Side/Juju Spirits.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Brewing
specs.description: Brew potions and poisons to heal and protect allies, destroy enemies, and invoke benevolent Loa Spirits.
specs.playstyle: A prep-and-pour healer: brew potions and protective concoctions before the pull, then dispense them as shields and restorative brews (shield-heavy kit: 5 ward effects vs 2 direct heals). The proactive healer archetype — your strength is what you bottled in advance, not panic buttons. The Cauldron system is literal: prepare Ingredients into a Cauldron and nearby allies drink the buff.

statPriority.pve: Spirit > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste > Intellect
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spirit > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Spirit is the true heal-scaling attribute here, not a bottom-of-list regen stat: Spirit Healer converts Spirit into both healing power and spell-crit chance, and the actual heals/shields carry direct Spirit coefficients (Frog Bones absorb Spirit, Healing Ward/Potion Toss/Splash all add SPI). So Spirit leads alongside Spell Power rather than trailing Intellect. Pure healer so spell-hit is correctly omitted; Crit stays a strong secondary for ward/brew payoff (Serpent's Guile, A Price To Pay), Haste mainly speeds Loa's Brew and periodic ticks. Intellect takes the badge only because Spirit isn't a badge option.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Shadowhunting
specs.description: Vol'jin's legacy: stalk in the shadows and engage with glaive-and-hex melee, weaving Shadow magic (11 shadow-school abilities) between Physical strikes. Plays like a skirmisher — dip in, burst, slip out.
specs.playstyle: Vol'jin's legacy: stalk in the shadows and engage with glaive-and-hex melee, weaving Shadow magic (11 shadow-school abilities) between Physical strikes. Plays like a skirmisher — dip in, burst, slip out.

statPriority.pve: Attack Power > Critical Strike > Agility > Intellect/Spirit > Spell Power > Ranged Hit (only until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Haste (lowest — it comes from Shadow Avatar/buffs, not gear)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Attack Power > Critical Strike > Agility > Ranged Hit (cap) > Haste
statPriority.note: Gears like a hunter: this is a ranged-weapon build (the Shadowhunter passive grants the ranged auto-shot) whose core abilities — Reclamation, Malefic Arrow, Spirit Glaive, Spirit Eclipse — all read ranged attack power, and Sen'jin's Apprentice pours your Intellect and Agility into ranged attack power on top, so raw Attack Power and Critical Strike (Eye of Kimbul applies on crit) lead. Ranged shots can't be dodged/parried, so no Expertise and only enough Hit to stop your specials missing; Haste is the lowest gear stat since it comes from Shadow Avatar and buffs, not gear.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Reclamation requires one.

classes.name: Felsworn
classes.summary: All Demon Hunters were once students under Illidan Stormrage, The Betrayer, and after his defeat at the Black Temple many abandoned his ways and rejoined the Horde and Alliance. Masters of glaive combat, anti-magic, and Fel power, they are an Alliance-only class until The Burning Crusade, when…

specs.name: Slayer
specs.description: Warglaive artistry: fast Physical strikes that feed Demonic Rage toward Demon Form. The bread-and-butter Felsworn DPS line — build rage, transform, shred. Fel shields in the kit give it surprising self-sufficiency.
specs.playstyle: Warglaive artistry: fast Physical strikes that feed Demonic Rage toward Demon Form. The bread-and-butter Felsworn DPS line — build rage, transform, shred. Fel shields in the kit give it surprising self-sufficiency.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until dual-wield white/special swings stop missing) > Expertise (until attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit (dual-wield cap) > Expertise (until attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration
statPriority.note: Physical DW spec scaling off AP-from-Agility (Azzinoth's Assault attack power, Oblivion attack power+Agility; Felcaked Blades/Spite add Agi). The kit is heavily crit-proc-driven — Dancing Blades (crit → off-hand AoE), Twin Fury (crit → Felfury), Chaotic (crit → Energy refund), You Cheat Gul'dan (crit-dmg) — so Crit must sit above Haste and Armor Pen, not below ArmPen as previously listed. PvP order re-fronted with Resilience > Stamina.

weapon.note: One-hand melee — Annihilan Strike requires a one-hander (dual-wield or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Infernal
specs.description: Fel magic annihilation — the caster-leaning Felsworn line (Fire 13 / Shadow 15 school mix). Built around Felstrike procs and fel eruptions; a pure ranged Felfury builder-spender whose Fel Fireball filler auto-procs Felstrikes — you react to what the fel gives you.
specs.playstyle: Fel magic annihilation — the caster-leaning Felsworn line (Fire 13 / Shadow 15 school mix). Built around Felstrike procs and fel eruptions; a pure ranged Felfury builder-spender whose Fel Fireball filler auto-procs Felstrikes — you react to what the fel gives you.

statPriority.pve: Spirit > Critical Strike > Intellect > Spell Power > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spirit > Critical Strike > Intellect > Haste
statPriority.note: Now a pure ranged Felfury builder-spender — no manual melee; Felstrikes proc automatically off your Fel Fireball casts. Spirit leads because it scales spell damage (Hidden Power converts Intellect AND Spirit into spell damage) and crit (Nether Spirit), so it's a true throughput stat here, not just regen — keep Spell Hit capped, then Spirit > Critical Strike > Intellect > Haste (bisbeard: Spirit ~2.17 dominant, Crit ~1.3 above Intellect ~0.66).

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Tyrant
specs.description: Bolster your physical form with demonic power: the Tyrant line leans hardest into Demon Form itself (Shadow-dominant kit), turning transformation windows into tanky, sustained-burst phases. You plan around your form uptime.
specs.playstyle: Bolster your physical form with demonic power: the Tyrant line leans hardest into Demon Form itself (Shadow-dominant kit), turning transformation windows into tanky, sustained-burst phases. You plan around your form uptime.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste > Dodge Rating (fuels self-buff scaling)
statPriority.pvp: Hit (cap) > Expertise (cap) > Resilience > Stamina > Agility > Dodge Rating (Fel Quickness/Demonscale/Infernal Alacrity mitigation+scaling) > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Damage still comes from Physical/Shadowflame melee hits (Stomp, Vengeance, Crashing Shadows scale off AP/SP) so Agility leads for AP+crit+innate dodge, but the whole self-bolster kit (Demonic Fortitude, Fel Quickness, Trickery, Demonscale) explicitly multiplies off Dodge Rating, making it worth valuing above pure Crit/Haste, more so in PvP where the extra avoidance also keeps this bruiser alive.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Carve requires a two-hander.

classes.name: Witch Hunter
classes.summary: Witch Hunters use a deadly combination of shadow magic and weapons to vanquish evil, often living in solitude and only roused to action when times are dire. Switching between dual crossbows and a flaming blade, they are the ultimate monster killers, sought after across the world for their prowess.

specs.name: Boltslinger
specs.description: Dual-crossbow skirmishing: near-pure Physical sustained fire while you maneuver the battlefield. The class's Weapon Swapping feature means you flow between bolts and blades — Boltslinger is the ranged stance perfected.
specs.playstyle: Dual-crossbow skirmishing: near-pure Physical sustained fire while you maneuver the battlefield. The class's Weapon Swapping feature means you flow between bolts and blades — Boltslinger is the ranged stance perfected.

statPriority.pve: Hit (ranged special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target), per Brimming Hatred's own +3% hit) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried, Brimming Hatred also grants +10) > Agility (RAP/crit/dodge; Silverhead Bolts additionally converts Agility into spell damage for the Twilight-school bolts) > Armor Penetration (stacks with Lucky Shot's built-in 25% armor ignore) > Critical Strike (Brimming Hatred/Lucky Shot both key off crits for rage and armor-shred) > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience/Stamina (squishy kiter with no baked-in mitigation) > Hit > Expertise > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste (mobility/burst windows, backed by Swoop/Vault/Daring Escape kiting kit)
statPriority.note: Near-pure Physical dual-crossbow sustained fire built around bolts and crits generating Rage, so the ranged special-attack hit/expertise caps (explicitly buffed by Brimming Hatred) come before Agility's RAP/crit payoff.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Damnation requires one.

specs.name: Houndmaster
specs.description: The dark-utility line (DB line name: Darkness): shadow tools, hunt marks and hound-handling that aid the whole party's damage. Plays as a pressure-support — you mark, harry and let the pack feast.
specs.playstyle: The dark-utility line (DB line name: Darkness): shadow tools, hunt marks and hound-handling that aid the whole party's damage. Plays as a pressure-support — you mark, harry and let the pack feast.

statPriority.pve: Hit (ranged special-attack cap, until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility (base RAP + crit that the whole ranged attack power kit scales on) > Intellect (Shadewalker converts Int into AP and Dark Mind converts Int into crit rating) > Spell Power (feeds the minor Shadow portions of Darkflock/Feast of Talons) > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience/Stamina (squishy pet-support ranged with no baked-in mitigation) > Hit > Expertise > Agility > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Ranged-weapon spec: 9 of its abilities scale on ranged attack power/attack power with only Darkflock (spell power) and Feast (spell power) leaning on Spell Power, so Agility (native ranged AP) is the primary damage stat. Intellect is a strong secondary because Shadewalker (AP = % of Intellect) and Dark Mind (crit rating = % of Intellect) convert it, but it does not leapfrog the Agility that supplies base AP.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Quickdraw requires one.

specs.name: Black Knight
specs.description: The dark knight, read from its full builder tree: two-handed strikes that scale with your parry rating, Noctis Blade healing you for 150% of its damage, doubled armor and Shadow Brands on everything you hit. A self-healing duelist that turns defense into offense — comfortably flexes toward tanking.
specs.playstyle: The dark knight, read from its full builder tree: two-handed strikes that scale with your parry rating, Noctis Blade healing you for 150% of its damage, doubled armor and Shadow Brands on everything you hit. A self-healing duelist that turns defense into offense — comfortably flexes toward tanking.

statPriority.pve: Hit (melee special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target), keeps Slash/Pommel Smash/Noctis Blade connecting) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility (AP, crit, and passive dodge) > Parry Rating (scales Noctis Blade's damage AND its 150%-of-damage self-heal - it's an active strike, not an avoidance proc - and Witchmail/Enchanted Armor double armor-from-items, so parry doubles as offense, sustain and mitigation) > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience/Stamina (a squishy duelist with no baked-in damage mitigation) > Hit > Expertise > Parry Rating (sustains the Noctis Blade lifesteal loop that lets it out-trade) > Agility > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Kit is explicitly parry-scaling - Noctis Blade is an active strike whose damage and big self-heal both scale with your parry RATING (not gated on dodging/parrying), and Witchmail/Enchanted Armor stack armor-from-items - so parry sits alongside the usual melee hit/expertise/Agility order instead of being pure DPS-only stacking.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Burrow Bolt requires one.

specs.name: Inquisition
specs.description: Use a flaming blade to purge evil from the world.
specs.playstyle: Purge evil with a flaming blade: melee strikes that layer Fire burns (11 fire-school abilities) over Physical damage. An ignite-and-execute rhythm — brand the target, then cut it down while it burns.

statPriority.pve: Hit (both the melee special-attack hit cap and the spell hit cap both matter - Smite Evil/Flourish are Physical while Fiery Judgment, Dawn Blade, Searing Blade, Burning Light, and Interrogate are Fire/Shadow casts) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Spell Power (Fiery Judgment, Dawn Blade, Searing Blade, Burning Light and Interrogate all carry an SP coefficient equal to or larger than their AP coefficient) > Agility (weapon damage, crit, dodge) > Critical Strike > Haste (extra swings plus faster Flourish/Searing Blade bleed ticks)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience/Stamina (squishy melee) > Hit > Spell Power > Expertise > Agility > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: A true dot-weave hybrid - flaming-blade Fire/Shadow burns layered on Physical weapon swings - so it uniquely needs both hit pools capped before Spell Power, which out-scales Agility across its own ability coefficients, takes over as the main damage stat.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Burrow Bolt requires one.

classes.name: Stormbringer
classes.summary: Young magi that lust for power and have little patience for study are often drawn to the path of the Stormbringer, though that power comes with volatility. Harnessing lightning in an arcane fashion distinct from shamans, these magi are explosively powerful and take command of storms for their own…

specs.name: Lightning
specs.description: Summon destructive lightning and electrical energy to obliterate enemies.
specs.playstyle: Destructive lightning built around the class's Static gamble — damage bonuses ramp at 70%/85% Static, but hitting 100% costs you 90% of your health and a stun. You ride the edge: the best Lightning players react to procs while managing how close to the line they dare sit.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit Rating (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Critical Strike Rating > Haste Rating > Intellect > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Spell Hit Rating (to cap) > Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Critical Strike Rating > Haste Rating
statPriority.note: Lightning's damage is almost entirely Nature/Arcane spellcasting off Spell Power (Arm of Thorim SP, Volt, Fluxblast), and its Static/Crackle proc engine is explicitly crit-fed (Destructive Force: crits generate Static; Crackling Surge/Voltaic Bursts add crit), so Hit-to-cap then Spell Power then Crit outrank Haste in this proc-driven single-target nuker.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Wind
specs.description: Use gale winds to disable and control enemies.
specs.playstyle: Gale-force control: knockbacks, slows and wind walls that disable and reposition enemies. You win by denying the enemy's plan — a control-support that shapes the battlefield before damage ever lands.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit Rating (to cap) > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste Rating > Critical Strike Rating > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Spell Hit Rating (to cap) > Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste Rating > Critical Strike Rating
statPriority.note: Wind is an Intellect spell-power caster (spell power on Aeroblast/Gale/Updraft/Surge of Might, spell power on Skyfall; Kiss of the Clouds shield scales off Intellect). Spell Power then Intellect must come before Haste; this proactive control/support kit barely invests in Crit (only ~5 crit passives), so Haste (more control/utility casts + extra Updraft ticks) ranks above Crit but below the primary attribute.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Maelstrom
specs.description: The storm unleashed: the kit is the class's AoE heart (15 storm/field effects — DB line name: Thunder), laced with Titan-gift empowerments from its alpha 'Gifts' ancestry. Drop storms, empower allies inside them, drown packs in rolling AoE.
specs.playstyle: The storm unleashed: the kit is the class's AoE heart (15 storm/field effects — DB line name: Thunder), laced with Titan-gift empowerments from its alpha 'Gifts' ancestry. Drop storms, empower allies inside them, drown packs in rolling AoE.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit Rating (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste Rating > Critical Strike Rating > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Spell Hit Rating (to cap) > Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste Rating > Critical Strike Rating
statPriority.note: Maelstrom is an Intellect spell-power AoE caster (spell power/spell power on Brine, Deluge, Drown, Eye of the Storm, Torrential Wrath, Stormfury), with Intellect explicitly converted into spell power (Tempest Aegis), haste (Whirling Maelstrom) and armor (Storm Channeling). Spell Power then Intellect lead; because the kit is channeled/ticking storm fields, Haste (faster ticks, and it scales Int→haste) ranks above Crit but still below the primary attribute.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Knight of Xoroth
classes.summary: Knights of Xoroth are dark experiments created by powerful Xorothian sorcerers, who possessed Azeroth's mightiest warriors with demonic spirits to forge a hellfire-wreathed legion. Warlocks later freed some of these Knights, who rejoined the Horde and Alliance; a Horde-only class until The Burning…

specs.name: War
specs.description: Tear your enemies apart with a weapon, siphoning Deathfire Orbs from them to empower your attacks.
specs.playstyle: Tear enemies apart and siphon Deathfire Orbs from their wounds (6 bleed/burn effects). The orb economy is the spec: wounds generate orbs, orbs fuel your strongest hits. Methodical, wound-stacking melee.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Strength > Hit (to cap) > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: 2H %-weapon/attack power striker (Skulltaker/Gore/Meatsaw/Consuming Blade) with Str->ArPen and Str->haste conversions (Superheated Blade, Render) and heavy crit talents, so Str/AP/ArPen behind the melee hit/expertise caps in PvE. PvP unchanged in scaling; only reordered so survivability (Resilience > Stamina) leads.

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

specs.name: Hellfire
specs.description: Take full command over Hellish, fiery demonic powers to burn enemies alive.
specs.playstyle: Full command of hellish fire: burn-heavy casting (6 DoT effects, Fire-leaning school mix) from a knight's chassis. Ignite everything, keep the burns rolling, and detonate orb-fueled payoffs.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Strength > Critical Strike > Haste > Intellect
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Strength > Spell Hit (to cap) > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Fire/Shadowflame DoT-weave caster (Seeking Flame, Hellmaw, Calamity all spell power/spell power scaling), but Burning Power converts Strength into spell damage and Rage Demon converts Strength into spell hit, while Cinderblade turns Intellect into crit/AP. So Strength is the primary attribute behind Spell Power and the spell-hit cap. (Prior note cited a nonexistent 'Battle Warlock'; the actual passives are Burning Power/Rage Demon/Cinderblade.) PvP reordered to lead with Resilience > Stamina.

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

specs.name: Defiance
specs.description: Use demonic influence to bolster your resolve in order to defend yourself and your allies.
specs.playstyle: Demonic influence as armor: the kit is the class's defensive wall — 8 shield/ward effects layered over fire-and-shadow retaliation. An absorb-tank that answers spikes with fel barriers and burns attackers back.

statPriority.pve: Defense rating (to crit-immunity) > Stamina > Dodge > Parry > Block > Strength > Armor
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Defense > Dodge/Parry > Strength > Block
statPriority.note: A block/absorb shield-tank whose threat, block value, and self-heals scale off Strength and Stamina (Oath of Defiance, Defiance Stats, Shieldgore, Suffuse), so crit immunity via Defense and a deep Stamina pool come first, then avoidance and block, with Strength for threat/mitigation.

weapon.note: The game defines no weapon requirement for this spec, so we don’t guess a weapon type.

classes.name: Guardian
classes.summary: The guardian is a valiant warrior, often of noble background, who is non-magical but aims to put themselves on the front line and defend the weak from evil. They are a tanking-focused warrior class with the best damage mitigation in the game.

specs.name: Vanguard
specs.description: The realm's most classical tank: 7 shield effects, 6 mitigation cooldowns, double taunt. Nearly pure Physical — you stand in front, rotate defensives against what the boss just did, and simply do not move. The safest tank pick in COA. Formations are the identity — Tower/Line Formation cuts your damage taken 10% while held.
specs.playstyle: The realm's most classical tank: 7 shield effects, 6 mitigation cooldowns, double taunt. Nearly pure Physical — you stand in front, rotate defensives against what the boss just did, and simply do not move. The safest tank pick in COA. Formations are the identity — Tower/Line Formation cuts your damage taken 10% while held.

statPriority.pve: Defense (to crit-immunity) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried, prevents dodge parry-haste) > Stamina (Bulwark converts Stamina into hit rating; Iron Guardian scales max HP) > Block Rating/Value > Strength (native Attack Power for threat) > Dodge/Parry > Armor
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Defense/Block > Strength > Armor
statPriority.note: Vanguard is an AP-scaled tank: every hit (attack power) plus a fat block/mitigation tree. Its only stat-conversion is Bulwark turning STAMINA into hit rating (the prior note's 'Defender of the Meek converts Strength into block+hit' is a misread — no such ability exists and hit comes from Stamina). So it's a Defense-cap-then-Stamina/Block tank whose Strength is just raw threat AP, not a hit source.

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Bastion requires a shield.

specs.name: Inspiration
specs.description: Support your allies by inspiring them in battle or throwing down banners.
specs.playstyle: The class feature made into a spec: generate Inspiration that passively reduces your allies' damage taken, and spend it on banners and rally effects (11 buff abilities — the biggest support kit in the game). You are the reason the party's health bars move slower.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried, avoid dodges) > Strength (Attack Power for Ballad/Sunder/Song of Battle weapon-damage scaling) > Spirit (uniquely powers Soldier's/Spellcaster's/Hero's Decrees, Feats of Strength/Honor/Glory, and Song of Steel) > Critical Strike (Tempo/Powerful Voice stacking) > Haste > Stamina
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Strength > Spirit > Haste
statPriority.note: Inspiration still swings a weapon and scales its nukes (Ballad of the Conqueror/Dragonslayer, Song of Battle) off Attack Power like any melee, but its entire class-defining support kit explicitly reads Spirit for buff/heal magnitude, making Spirit a genuinely valuable pick unique to this spec instead of dead weight.

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Press the Attack requires a shield.

specs.name: Gladiator
specs.description: Slay with a great weapon from a tank's frame: heavy Physical swings with enough residual shields to shrug off mistakes. A bruiser — bring it when you want DPS that can eat a frontal and keep swinging.
specs.playstyle: Slay with a great weapon from a tank's frame: heavy Physical swings with enough residual shields to shrug off mistakes. A bruiser — bring it when you want DPS that can eat a frontal and keep swinging.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength (native Attack Power + Duty/One True Champion/Shield Training convert it into block rating) > Agility (Duty converts Agility into bonus Attack Power) > Armor Penetration (Dirty Tactics baseline +5%) > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Strength > Agility > Armor Penetration
statPriority.note: Every big Gladiator hit (Grand Entrance, Pulverize, Sever, Centurion Strike, Ram) is a flat attack power-scaled Physical nuke. The only offensive stat conversion is Duty turning AGILITY into Attack Power; every Strength passive (Shield Training, One True Champion, Vigilant Reflexes, Duty's) converts Strength into BLOCK RATING. The prior note's 'Guardian passive turns Strength into Critical Strike Rating' is a misread — no Str->crit conversion exists. Strength stays primary as native Attack Power (plus block), Agility is the secondary AP source, and Armor Pen (primed by Dirty Tactics) pays off once melee hit/expertise caps are met.

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Press the Attack requires a shield.

classes.name: Templar
classes.summary: Monks are holy warriors that often specialize in using their bare hands in the stead of any kind of traditional weapon. Today's monks are a mixture of Scarlet Crusade techniques and ancient teachings from long-forgotten civilizations.

specs.name: Zealot
specs.description: Bare-handed holy combat: fast strikes build the class's 10-Rune meter, inscribing a Rune that empowers your next 10 autos — unarmed play grants attack speed and Holy damage. A combo-flow melee where your damage comes in inscribed waves.
specs.playstyle: Bare-handed holy combat: fast strikes build the class's 10-Rune meter, inscribing a Rune that empowers your next 10 autos — unarmed play grants attack speed and Holy damage. A combo-flow melee where your damage comes in inscribed waves.

statPriority.pve: Attack Power (Calm Combatant scales Holy damage off AP; AP also drives the weapon damage Zealotry & Righteous Upheaval read) ≈ Agility (AP + crit) > Critical Strike > Spell Power > Haste — all after Hit Rating & Expertise to cap
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit Rating / Expertise (to cap) > Attack Power / Agility > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Dual-wield Oath builder-spender: Calm Combatant converts your Holy damage to scale off attack power, and AP also drives the weapon damage your Zealotry procs, Righteous Upheaval and auto attacks read — so you gear full AP (Agility first for the extra crit, then flat Attack Power). Spell Power is the hybrid half of most coefficients; take only enough Hit/Expertise to keep Oath builders from missing, since a miss stalls the whole rotation.

weapon.note: One-hand melee — Righteous Upheaval requires a one-hander (dual-wield or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Oathkeeper
specs.description: Holy power channelled into allies from melee range: ward-and-empower healing (3 shields, 5 defensives, Holy-heavy mix) rather than big direct heals. A frontline support-healer — you keep oaths, and oaths keep the party standing. Its signature staggers you: 40% of direct damage taken is delayed over 8 seconds — a monk-style smoothing layer under the wards.
specs.playstyle: Holy power channelled into allies from melee range: ward-and-empower healing (3 shields, 5 defensives, Holy-heavy mix) rather than big direct heals. A frontline support-healer — you keep oaths, and oaths keep the party standing. Its signature staggers you: 40% of direct damage taken is delayed over 8 seconds — a monk-style smoothing layer under the wards.

statPriority.pve: Hit Rating (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise Rating (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Stamina > Critical Strike > Haste > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Agility > Hit Rating (to cap) > Critical Strike > Expertise > Haste
statPriority.note: Oathkeeper is a weapon-swinging staff bruiser/off-healer whose damage rides Attack Power (Testament of Faith attack power, Temple Guardian attack power, Reckoning/Armor of Faith attack power) and whose Agility is the stacked/converted attribute (Bells of Light +Agi, Paragon Sta+Agi%, Consecrated Armor armor=%Agi, Graceful Fighter parry=%Agi), so it's a Physical/Agility spec — not the Intellect/Spell-Power caster the old data claimed. Hit/Expertise to land the OathBreakers, then Agility-derived AP, with Stamina weighted high because Benediction/Reckoning heals scale off Stamina.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Reckoning requires a two-hander.

specs.name: Crusader
specs.description: Runes inscribed onto steel: weapon-armed Templar play that trades Zealot's speed for heavier, rune-empowered strikes with Holy after-shocks and modest self-healing. Steady, durable crusading melee.
specs.playstyle: Runes inscribed onto steel: weapon-armed Templar play that trades Zealot's speed for heavier, rune-empowered strikes with Holy after-shocks and modest self-healing. Steady, durable crusading melee.

statPriority.pve: Hit Rating (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise Rating (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration > Spell Power
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit Rating (to cap) > Agility > Critical Strike > Expertise > Haste
statPriority.note: Crusader trades Zealot's speed for heavier weapon-based rune strikes (Tyr's Hammer, Vindication, Titanstrike) that still hinge on Hit/Expertise to connect and scale primarily off Agility-derived AP with a Holy SP kicker, and its dense crit-synergy talents (Scarlet Champion, Powerful Strikes, One with Light) favor Crit over Haste for its slower, bigger-hit rhythm.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Argent Blade requires a two-hander.

classes.name: Bloodmage
classes.summary: The Sons of Arugal are past minions of Archmage Arugal, afflicted with the Curse of the Worgen. The Sons that have returned to the Horde and the Alliance have gained some level of control over their curse, and can hold back from feasting on the flesh of their allies. (Alliance-only class.)

specs.name: Sanguine
specs.description: Nightmare and Blood magic (Fire 17/Shadow 22 school mix): drain-style health-cost casting that builds Rage and Thirst while sustaining yourself off your own damage. The caster face of the Bloodmage — proactive drains, self-fueling.
specs.playstyle: Nightmare and Blood magic (Fire 17/Shadow 22 school mix): drain-style health-cost casting that builds Rage and Thirst while sustaining yourself off your own damage. The caster face of the Bloodmage — proactive drains, self-fueling.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target, hit-reliant Shadow/Fire dot-weave) > Spell Power > Haste (faster Hematophage/Sanguine Rupture ticks and Thirst-reduced Valanar's Vengeance casts) > Critical Strike (Blood Parasites/Atherann's Wickedness/A Pointed Death all pay off crit) > Spirit/Stamina (fuel Cunning of Atherann's Stam+Spi spell-damage conversion and pay the health costs on Valanar's Vengeance/Keleseth's Calamity/Sanguine Rupture) > Intellect (only matters via Cunning of Atherann's 15%-of-Int crit conversion — no mana pool is spent)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina (drain-tank kit spends its own health on casts, needs the buffer) > Spell Hit (cap) > Spell Power > Spirit > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Every core nuke (Hematophage, Vampiric Fang, Valanar's Vengeance, Keleseth's Calamity) scales off Spell Power and costs Rage or raw health rather than mana, so hit-to-land and SP lead while Intellect's usual mana-pool role is moot.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Accursed
specs.description: The curse embraced: lurk, transform, and tear targets to shreds in Accursed Form (heavy Physical kit built around bleeds). You build Rage with human-form bolts and spend it in transformed windows — build human, spend wolf.
specs.playstyle: The curse embraced: lurk, transform, and tear targets to shreds in Accursed Form (heavy Physical kit built around bleeds). You build Rage with human-form bolts and spend it in transformed windows — build human, spend wolf.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target, direct-hit strikes like Reave/Ravenous Strike/Puncturing Fangs must land) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried for your Accursed Form burst windows) > Agility (Accursed Form literally converts 50% of Agility into AP) > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike (Killer Instinct/Ultra Instinct/Darkcasting are all crit-fed) > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit/Expertise (still must connect to open Accursed Form windows) > Agility > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: A builder-spender kit (build Rage with your bolts, spend it in Accursed Form's free melee windows, with bleed effects layered throughout) whose form explicitly converts 50% of Agility into AP, so Agility plus reliable direct-hit connection (Hit/Expertise) outrank the crit chain it's built around.

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

specs.name: Eternal
specs.description: Use the Worgen curse to your advantage: a self-healing tank line (heal + HoT + defensives) that bolsters packmates while it holds the line. Less about flat mitigation, more about regenerating through damage and making the pack tougher around you.
specs.playstyle: Use the Worgen curse to your advantage: a self-healing tank line (heal + HoT + defensives) that bolsters packmates while it holds the line. Less about flat mitigation, more about regenerating through damage and making the pack tougher around you.

statPriority.pve: Defense (to the crit-immunity cap, non-negotiable floor) > Stamina (Eternal Curse grants +25% total Stamina and +200% armor-from-items, so Stamina is doubly amplified) > Hit/Expertise (Bare Fangs/Blood Howl taunts and Bloodfang Bite/Rotclaw threat abilities must land) > Armor > Agility (drives AP for the threat-generating Ravenous Bite/Claw Sweep kit) > Critical Strike/Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Defense > Armor > Hit/Expertise
statPriority.note: This is a self-healing 'curse-tank' (Ravenous Bite heals, Ironhide delays damage, Eternal Curse pumps armor and Stamina) rather than a pure-avoidance tank, so after the mandatory Defense floor it leans hardest into the Stamina/armor multipliers its own transformation grants, with landed taunts/threat abilities next.

weapon.note: The game defines no weapon requirement for this spec, so we don’t guess a weapon type.

specs.name: Fleshweaver
specs.description: The sacrifice healer, confirmed by its full tree: spells that cost your own health build Pooled Vitality stacks, Vampyr's Kiss bleeds feed the engine, and Lana'thel's vampiric blessings convert Spirit into power. You literally spend yourself to keep others alive — the highest-skill-ceiling healer in COA.
specs.playstyle: The sacrifice healer, confirmed by its full tree: spells that cost your own health build Pooled Vitality stacks, Vampyr's Kiss bleeds feed the engine, and Lana'thel's vampiric blessings convert Spirit into power. You literally spend yourself to keep others alive — the highest-skill-ceiling healer in COA.

statPriority.pve: Spell Power (every heal — Hemoglobe, Sanguine Mend, Dark Liturgy, Crimson Tide — scales directly off SP) > Spirit (Teachings of Lana'thel converts Spirit straight into spell hit and spell crit rating, on top of Hemoglobe/Aortic Aegis's own Spi coefficients) > Stamina (pays for the health-cost casts — Gorge, Suture, Vampyr's Kiss, Dark Liturgy — and co-scales Hemoglobe) > Haste (more frequent Hemoglobe pulses/Crimson Tide windows in a dot-transmute healing style) > Critical Strike > Intellect (no mana is ever spent, so Int's only value is incidental)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Spirit > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: A pure sacrifice-healer whose costs are Rage and raw health rather than mana, so Spell Power and the Spirit that both fuels its Sta/Spi healing coefficients and converts into hit/crit rating outrank Intellect entirely.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Ranger
classes.summary: Rangers are a basic group of people who make use of simple weapons, either dual one-handed weapons or a ranged weapon, using them to their maximum capacity in place of where others may use magic. Rangers specialize in being highly skilled archers or assassins, and some, depending on their…

specs.name: Archery
specs.description: Use a bow or crossbow to rapid fire shots from afar.
specs.playstyle: Rapid fire from afar, run on Combo Points (the class's Archer style): build with quick shots, spend on aimed payoffs. Clean, methodical ranged play with Hide-crafted buffs from the class kit smoothing the downtime.

statPriority.pve: Hit Rating (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Agility > Critical Strike > Armor Penetration > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit Rating (to cap) > Agility > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Pure-Physical bow builder-spender: crits fund the Advantage loop (Archery Master generates an extra stack on Quick Shot crits; Double The Pace/Devastating Shots grant crit), so Crit sits above ArP/Haste. Hit lands the shots that start the loop. Expertise omitted (doesn't apply to ranged). PvP order corrected to lead with Resilience > Stamina per survivability-first ordering.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Brutal Shot requires one.

specs.name: Brigand
specs.description: Dual-weapon close-quarters fighting — the Ranger who'd rather duel than snipe. Almost pure Physical with light poison accents; combo-driven melee tempo with a skirmisher's escape tools.
specs.playstyle: Dual-weapon close-quarters fighting — the Ranger who'd rather duel than snipe. Almost pure Physical with light poison accents; combo-driven melee tempo with a skirmisher's escape tools.

statPriority.pve: Hit Rating (dual-wield white hit (until swings land), specials to the special-attack hit cap) > Expertise Rating (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit/Expertise (to caps) > Agility > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Dual-wield duelist whose attack power finishers (Assault, Ravage, Viper's Bite) rely on landed Physical hits and whose passives punish being dodged/parried (Beatdown, Blade Twisting), so both hit caps precede Agility scaling. PvP order corrected to lead with Resilience > Stamina per survivability-first ordering.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Cutthroat requires one.

specs.name: Farstrider
specs.description: Knowledge of the wilds turned into party value: traps, trail-craft, protective tonics and mobility for the whole group. The utility Ranger — you make every route safer and every pull start on your terms.
specs.playstyle: Knowledge of the wilds turned into party value: traps, trail-craft, protective tonics and mobility for the whole group. The utility Ranger — you make every route safer and every pull start on your terms.

statPriority.pve: Hit Rating (to land proc/utility triggers) > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit Rating > Agility > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Party value comes from Physical/Nature abilities whose crits spawn Dream Flowers and whose Agility scaling directly buffs allies (Rally The Archers, Whipping Pressure, Affinity), so Hit and Agility/Crit drive both personal throughput and group buffs. PvP order corrected to lead with Resilience > Stamina per survivability-first ordering.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Emerald Arrow requires one.

classes.name: Chronomancer
classes.summary: Chronomancers are magi with a mastery over the manipulation of time and the nature of the universe. They are highly intellectual and very uncommon, descended from the ancient Order of the Chronomagi who once studied under the Bronze Dragonflight.

specs.name: Infinite
specs.description: Chaotic time magic as a weapon (Arcane-heavy, the class's biggest kit): spend the class's 4 Order/Chaos Charges on combination payoffs and ride what the timeline gives you. Reactive, proc-chasing caster DPS with echoes of every school.
specs.playstyle: Chaotic time magic as a weapon (Arcane-heavy, the class's biggest kit): spend the class's 4 Order/Chaos Charges on combination payoffs and ride what the timeline gives you. Reactive, proc-chasing caster DPS with echoes of every school.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Intellect > Critical Strike > Spirit > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (to cap) > Spell Power > Intellect > Critical Strike > Spirit > Haste
statPriority.note: Arcane/chromatic caster: every hit scales off Spell Power, so Hit-to-cap then Spell Power lead. Intellect is a real throughput stat (Chrono Sorcery converts it to spell damage). Crit multiplies the Order/Chaos-charge finishers, and Spirit is NOT dead weight here -- Gift of the Infinite Dragonflight converts Spirit into both spell damage and spell hit (Temporal Swiftness also converts it to haste), so it belongs in the priority above Haste rather than being omitted.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Time
specs.description: Rule-bending utility and support abilities that can empower you or your allies.
specs.playstyle: The rewind healer: restore order by turning wounds back along their own timeline (3 heals, 4 shields, Holy-leaning mix). The most reactive healer in COA — damage happens, and you make it not have happened. Bring it when spike damage is the problem.

statPriority.pve: Spirit > Spell Power > Haste > Intellect > Critical Strike > MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spirit > Spell Power > Haste > Intellect > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Rewind healer: heal/shield formulas scale off Spell Power, but Sands of Life converts Spirit DIRECTLY into healing power (Rippling Power and Temporal Restoration reinforce it), so Spirit is the top throughput stat, not just sustain. Haste compresses spike-coverage casts; Intellect and Crit are close and can be swapped to taste (Crit shaves Ripple's cooldown via A Ripple In Time and feeds Cadence of Time). Pure healing — no spell-hit line, so Hit/Expertise are not itemized.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Artificer
specs.description: Clockwork support: deploy Bronze and Infinite Protector constructs and run a 7-buff empowerment engine for the party. You build your value before the pull — constructs out, buffs rolling — then maintain the machine.
specs.playstyle: Clockwork support: deploy Bronze and Infinite Protector constructs and run a 7-buff empowerment engine for the party. You build your value before the pull — constructs out, buffs rolling — then maintain the machine.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (to cap) > Spell Penetration (to cap) > Spirit > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power > Intellect
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spirit > Spell Hit (to cap) > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power > Intellect
statPriority.note: Hit is the scarcest, highest-value stat — there are no hit talents in the tree and it's thin on Spirit-itemized gear — so cap it first; spell penetration is worth capping so your Arcane portions (Shatter Echo, Singularity Core, Decomposition) land on higher-level targets. Spirit is the throughput backbone (Wand Expert converts it to ranged attack power; Artificer's Empowerment raises it). Spell Power is overrated — only a few abilities scale it and weakly — so treat ~100-200 as a floor, not a priority. Reflects the Jul 2026 expert audit (data/coa_player_reports.json pr-0019/pr-0020).

weapon.note: Wand-primary ranged DPS, not a spell-power caster: Artificer's Wand, Singularity Core and Shatter Echo all scale off Ranged Weapon damage, so your wand's listed damage range is a core stat. Artificer's Wand has a static cast speed, so wand speed/DPS are irrelevant — take the highest flat damage range, which means a slow wand. Main-hand slot is unsettled: Singularity Core is currently bugged to pull from your melee main-hand's damage, so a high-damage two-hander is temporarily best there; players expect a fix reverting it to ranged-weapon scaling, after which a fast dagger is favored over a slow two-hander. Treat the main-hand pick as in flux until that resolves (as of 2026-07).

classes.name: Necromancer
classes.summary: The Necromancer is a form of mage specialising in the dark arts. Many tend toward a less-than-good alignment and most served the Lich King before his fall; now rejoining the Horde and Alliance, they spread terrible plagues, conjure icy magic, and summon legions of undead to overwhelm their enemies.

specs.name: Death
specs.description: Afflict your foes with terrible plagues and destroy them with shadow magics. Most Death abilities do not consume Runic Power.
specs.playstyle: The plague spec — 17 distinct rot/plague/decay effects, the densest DoT kit in COA. Load every plague you own onto the target and watch the attrition math win. Maximum proactive: your damage was decided 10 seconds ago.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Haste Rating > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Intellect > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (cap) > Haste Rating > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Intellect
statPriority.note: 17 stacking plagues/DoTs whose damage was 'decided 10 seconds ago' need Hit to actually land, and the spec's own passive ("periodic damage spells now benefit from spell haste") plus a wall of haste-proc talents (Angel of Death, Blightweaver, Mutation's faster ticks) make Haste an unusually strong secondary for squeezing extra plague ticks ahead of raw Spell Power and Crit.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Rime
specs.description: Killing cold: direct Frost nukes with freeze/CC setups (3 control effects) and AoE shatters. The immediate-gratification Necromancer — cast it, it dies now, and packs get frozen before they reach you.
specs.playstyle: Killing cold: direct Frost nukes with freeze/CC setups (3 control effects) and AoE shatters. The immediate-gratification Necromancer — cast it, it dies now, and packs get frozen before they reach you.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste Rating > Intellect > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (cap) > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste Rating > Intellect
statPriority.note: A burst-cooldown direct-nuke spec built around Deathchill/shatter payoffs, capped by Glacial Impact explicitly scaling with 'critical strike rating' and being guaranteed to crit, so Crit is prioritized right behind Spell Power once Hit is capped for the big single-target hits.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Animation
specs.description: The summoner: 15 raise/animate effects command an undead retinue that protects you and feasts on your enemies. Army management is the gameplay — position, sacrifice, resummon. Your damage walks on its own legs.
specs.playstyle: The summoner: 15 raise/animate effects command an undead retinue that protects you and feasts on your enemies. Army management is the gameplay — position, sacrifice, resummon. Your damage walks on its own legs.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target, for self-cast Crypt Swarm/Ghoulify) > Spell Power > Haste Rating > Intellect > Critical Strike
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (cap) > Spell Power > Haste Rating > Intellect > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Nearly every minion tooltip (Bone Wraith, Abomination, Ghoul, Rotling) is written as SP*X (often plus Int*X), so Spell Power is the master damage multiplier for the whole army, while Haste shortens the Animate/Raise cast times and speeds Crypt Swarm ticks to keep the summon rotation and army uptime high — Crit has comparatively little kit support and sits last.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Pyromancer
classes.summary: Pyromancers are mages that have devoted their studies to the school of Fire. Some have a draconic lineage that allows them to harness the power of the dragonflights in battle; others are simple fire magi who use their power to incinerate foes. (Class Leader: Kael'thas Sunstrider.)

specs.name: Incineration
specs.description: Turn single enemies to ash in an instant with super-heated fire attacks and damage-over-time.
specs.playstyle: Turn one enemy to ash: super-heated single-target casting that stacks heat into ignition windows. The boss-killer Pyromancer — plant your feet, ramp the burn, melt the target.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (cap) > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power
statPriority.note: Fire-SP caster whose L15/L30 passives convert Intellect straight into spell damage, spell haste, and spell crit, so Intellect (the primary attribute) leads the secondaries right after the spell-hit cap; Volcanic Flames + Flamecasting make Crit/Haste snowball, hence Crit ahead of Haste. PvP fronts Resilience > Stamina.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Flameweaving
specs.description: Weaving fire through the class's combo system (Flame Orb, Path of Flames): DoT-laced AoE that rewards routing flames through as many targets as possible — with a Nature-school thread (15 abilities) that hints at flame-tending utility. The pack-clearing Pyromancer.
specs.playstyle: Weaving fire through the class's combo system (Flame Orb, Path of Flames): DoT-laced AoE that rewards routing flames through as many targets as possible — with a Nature-school thread (15 abilities) that hints at flame-tending utility. The pack-clearing Pyromancer.

statPriority.pve: Spirit > Intellect > Critical Strike > Spell Power > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spirit > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power
statPriority.note: Pure healer, so spell hit is dropped from the PvE order. The L15 passive converts Spirit directly into healing spell power and Ember Touch/Cinderheart/Kindle/Roaring Pyre/Phoenix Dive all scale off Spirit, so Spirit edges out Intellect as the top primary (bisbeard ~0.54 vs ~0.46), with Critical Strike the standout secondary (bisbeard's highest weight ~1.3). PvP fronts Resilience > Stamina.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Draconic
specs.description: Harness your draconic lineage to deliver fast, short-ranged Fire attacks, and build Breath Charges to unleash your breath weapon.
specs.playstyle: Draconic lineage in close: fast, short-ranged Fire attacks (36 fire abilities) with dragon-scale defensives and the Phoenix at your side. A battle-caster that fights inside the danger zone and is built to survive it.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (cap) > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power > Stamina
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (cap) > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power
statPriority.note: PvE order was already correct (Intellect leads after the hit cap, then Crit > Haste) because the L15 passive turns Intellect into spell damage, crit, and hit and Dragon's Edge/crits generate Heat. Only fix: the PvP order now fronts Resilience > Stamina per the priority rule (previously Stamina > Resilience behind spell hit).

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Cultist
classes.summary: Above all else, Cultists are devoted to the four Old Gods. Some have formally renounced their dark masters, but many remain skeptical; regardless, their power cannot be denied, and they have been allowed back into the Horde and Alliance if they agree to denounce the Old Gods.

specs.name: Godblade
specs.description: Invoke the powers of the Old Gods to destroy your enemies by corrupting their minds and assaulting them with Eldritch horrors.
specs.playstyle: An Old God's edge: melee strikes that channel void corruption (Shadow 32 vs Physical 11 — a caster wearing a swordsman's stance) while the class's Insanity ticks toward madness at 100 stacks. Hybrid combo play with a built-in doomsday clock.

statPriority.pve: Hit (melee special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target)) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit (to cap) > Expertise > Strength > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Its weapon-damage-based melee specials (Netherstrike, Hammer of Twilight, Entropic Slam/Strike, Reaving Blade) need the special-attack hit and dodge caps met first, after which Strength is doubly valuable because Void Strength converts it into both Shadow spell damage and spell hit for the class's off-school Shadow procs.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Netherstrike requires a two-hander.

specs.name: Corruption
specs.description: Summon powers from the infinite void to annihilate enemies with blasts of shadowy magic.
specs.playstyle: Void annihilation from range: corruption DoTs punctuated by tentacle and Eldritch Horror summons. Classic warlock-descended gameplay — afflict, summon, drain — with Insanity management as the spice.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit (to cap) > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: A pure Shadow dot-weave/burst caster (C'Thun's Power scales damage off Intellect) whose kit explicitly rewards crit (Lightbreaker +50% crit damage, Wicked Intentions, Madness stacking) and whose instant-cast spam and DoT ticks (Abyssal Decay, Darkwither) benefit from haste after hit is capped.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Dreadnought
specs.description: The void sword-and-board, confirmed by its full tree: Twilight Shieldtoss bounces cursed shields, Adaptive Combatant stacks parry/dodge, and Malignant Armor converts every 100 armor into attack power. A classic avoidance tank whose defense literally becomes its damage.
specs.playstyle: The void sword-and-board, confirmed by its full tree: Twilight Shieldtoss bounces cursed shields, Adaptive Combatant stacks parry/dodge, and Malignant Armor converts every 100 armor into attack power. A classic avoidance tank whose defense literally becomes its damage.

statPriority.pve: Defense (to crit-immunity) > Stamina > Dodge/Parry Rating > Strength > Block Rating > Armor
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Defense > Dodge/Parry > Strength > Armor
statPriority.note: A void avoidance/absorb tank. Survival stats come first: cap Defense for crit-immunity, then Stamina (its Void-Enhanced Shield absorb scales off Stamina, as does its slam damage), then avoidance from Adaptive Combatant (flat parry+dodge). Strength matters for threat because Strength of the Sha (L20) converts Strength into Shadow spell damage and its Shadow-spell threat is doubled by Twilight Domain. There is no Strength-to-Block-Rating or armor-to-attack-power conversion in the kit; the prior note invented those.

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Twilight Shieldtoss requires a shield.

specs.name: Heretic
specs.description: Heal by the Old Gods' influence (DB line name: Influence): shield-forward dark mending — 6 ward effects against 4 DoTs, wrapping allies in whispers instead of light. A proactive absorb-healer with a damage thread woven through it.
specs.playstyle: Heal by the Old Gods' influence (DB line name: Influence): shield-forward dark mending — 6 ward effects against 4 DoTs, wrapping allies in whispers instead of light. A proactive absorb-healer with a damage thread woven through it.

statPriority.pve: Spell Power > Intellect > Haste > Critical Strike > Spirit/MP5 > Spell Hit (minor, for Malevolence's damage component)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: A shield-forward absorb healer whose ward/heal formulas scale directly off spell power / bonus healing (healing power, healing power) with Intellect as the secondary (Abyssal Protection's heal scales with Intellect and spell power). Haste speeds its ticking auras (Entropic Host, Armageddon) and short-cast heals, and Crit feeds Blessing of Yogg-Saron's on-crit empower. Its damage component needs little hit because Shadow Training makes its attacks unable to miss. There is no passive that converts Intellect into hit/expertise (the prior note's 'Herald of Yogg-Saron' conversion does not exist).

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

classes.name: Starcaller
classes.summary: The Starcaller is a heavily armed and heavily armoured warrior imbued with the power of the Moon and the Stars. Versatile and adaptive to different fighting styles, their disciplines range from followers of Elune and the Earthmother to Arcane-trained battle astronomers.

specs.name: Moon Guard
specs.description: Martial weapons imbued with Moon and Stars: the kit's 5 shields and 3 defensives over an Arcane-Physical melee core make this the Starcaller's frontline line — an astral warder who guards with moonlight barriers. Role read from the kit; verify in the builder.
specs.playstyle: Martial weapons imbued with Moon and Stars: the kit's 5 shields and 3 defensives over an Arcane-Physical melee core make this the Starcaller's frontline line — an astral warder who guards with moonlight barriers. Role read from the kit; verify in the builder.

statPriority.pve: Defense (crit-immune (far less Defense than retail at level 60)) > Stamina > Hit/Expertise (land Starsunder/Celestial Cleave threat swings) > Parry (feeds the Vengeance of Elune parry-proc plus Cosmic Vengeance/Lunar Blades/Starsweeper triggers) > Dodge > Block (Celestial Aegis, Blanket of Stars, Enchanted Aegis) > Agility (Attack Power via Virtue, block value) > Strength / Intellect (Moon Guard converts Strength 1:1 into Intellect, so they are effectively one stat here — fuelling the %-mana shields Moonlit Bulwark / Astral Reconstitution and the spell-power/max-mana damage scaling; a damage-leaning PvP build like Bigdaddy's itemizes heavily for Strength) > Critical Strike
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Defense/Dodge/Parry/Block (Blanket of Stars, Cosmic Crown) > Hit/Expertise > Agility > Intellect
statPriority.note: Five shield/block talents (Blanket of Stars, Cosmic Crown, Enchanted Aegis) and a parry-gated Vengeance of Elune push Defense-capped avoidance to the top, while the kit's %-mana barriers (Astral Armor, Moonlit Bulwark) make Intellect matter more than for a typical tank. Moon Guard converts Strength into Intellect 1:1, so Strength doubles as your damage/mana-shield stat; and with no PvP-power stat live in COA right now, high-stat crafted/blacksmith plate (e.g. Imperial Plate) can outvalue Resilience-bearing PvP gear for a damage-leaning build (per Bigdaddy).

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Blanket of Stars requires a shield.

specs.name: Sentinel
specs.description: Arrows enchanted with Elune's power: Arcane-and-Fire ranged volleys (the old 'Moonbow' school). Sustained celestial archery — starfall on a bowstring.
specs.playstyle: Arrows enchanted with Elune's power: Arcane-and-Fire ranged volleys (the old 'Moonbow' school). Sustained celestial archery — starfall on a bowstring.

statPriority.pve: Intellect (the master stat — Explosive Moonlight converts it into ranged attack power AND hit rating, Huntress Training converts it into haste, and Huntress Shot / Starfire Shot / Trueshot all scale off your maximum mana, so Intellect drives nearly every part of your damage) > Spell Power (Starcall, Rain of Comets, Lunar Lance, and the SP term on every shot) > Critical Strike (Eye of Elune plus the bonus crit damage on Starfire Shot) > Haste > Hit only until your shots stop missing a +3-level target (Explosive Moonlight already supplies a chunk of hit from Intellect) > Agility (a minor ranged-AP/crit contributor once Intellect covers attack power)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Intellect > Critical Strike > Haste > Spell Power > Agility
statPriority.note: Intellect-driven arcane archer: despite firing a ranged weapon, Intellect — not Agility — is the master stat. Explosive Moonlight turns Intellect into ranged attack power and hit, Huntress Training turns it into haste, and Huntress Shot / Starfire Shot / Trueshot scale off maximum mana (Intellect), so it gears like a caster, not an Agility hunter. Matches the community bisbeard weights (Intellect ≫ Agility).

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Arrow of the Goddess requires one.

specs.name: Moon Priest
specs.description: Water magic under moonlight (Arcane 21/Frost 19): flowing, tide-like restoration — the alpha 'Tides' healer under a priestly name. Keep the tide rolling over the party rather than spot-fixing single wounds.
specs.playstyle: Water magic under moonlight (Arcane 21/Frost 19): flowing, tide-like restoration — the alpha 'Tides' healer under a priestly name. Keep the tide rolling over the party rather than spot-fixing single wounds.

statPriority.pve: Spell Power > Haste (extra Torrent/Slipstream/Pond ticks, faster Deluge/Water Bolt casts) > Critical Strike (Scepter of the Moon, Moon's Embrace, Enchanted Water) > Intellect (mana pool + spell crit) > Spirit/MP5 (sustain through long tide-healing channels)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste > Intellect/Spirit
statPriority.note: Torrent, Slipstream, and Pond are HoT-style channels that reward Haste for extra ticks, while crit-triggered Lunar Phase talents (Scepter of the Moon, Moon's Embrace) and big single heals (Moonwater Blessing, Aegis of Neptulon) reward stacking Spell Power and Crit on top.

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

specs.name: Warden
specs.description: Corrected from the full tree: not a tank — an arcane blade-dancer. Fan of Knives and Starsunder crits build Scattered Stars stacks, Astral Blade spends mana for precision Arcane strikes. The night-elf assassin fantasy running on starlight instead of energy.
specs.playstyle: Corrected from the full tree: not a tank — an arcane blade-dancer. Fan of Knives and Starsunder crits build Scattered Stars stacks, Astral Blade spends mana for precision Arcane strikes. The night-elf assassin fantasy running on starlight instead of energy.

statPriority.pve: Hit (melee special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target)) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried, keeps Aspect of the Warden swings and Astral Blade/Sentinel Glaive landing) > Agility (Attack Power, crit) > Critical Strike (Shadowsong's Mandate proc engine, Scattered Stars crit application) > Spell Power (Astral Blade's dominant SP term) > Haste (more swings for Aspect of the Warden procs and faster Scattered Stars stacking)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit/Expertise > Agility > Critical Strike > Spell Power
statPriority.note: Astral Blade's SP coefficient outweighs its AP term even inside a weapon-swing builder-spender loop, so this crit-focused blade-dancer still wants Spell Power stacked alongside Hit/Expertise to land Aspect of the Warden procs and Shadowsong's Mandate crits.

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

classes.name: Sun Cleric
classes.summary: Sun Clerics are masters of Holy and Sun magic, priests visited by a powerful Valkyrie spirit said to hail from the Sun itself. As the ultimate war priests, some take the Valkyron Oath to fill their bodies with the strength of the sun and defend their allies from harm.

specs.name: Piety
specs.description: Smite evil with the searing light of the sun.
specs.playstyle: Smite evil with searing sunlight: Holy-Fire casting that stacks the class's Solar Power (small damage/heal bonus that lasts 2 minutes but never refreshes — keep earning it). The battle-priest face of the Sun Cleric.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target vs raid boss) > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Haste > Intellect > MP5/Spirit
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Spell Hit (cap) > Spell Power > Critical Strike > Stamina > Haste
statPriority.note: Its Holy/Fire nuke chain (Flash, Dawnsear, Solar Wrath, Sun Ray, Radiant Flame) is entirely Spell-Power-scaled with real cast/channel times, and its stack of crit-chance talents (Radiant Marks, Servant of the Light, Solar Incandescence, Ancient Etchings) reward Crit over Haste once hit is capped.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Blessings
specs.description: Restore your allies by calling upon the power of the Holy light.
specs.playstyle: The classic Holy healer of COA: direct restoration by the power of the Light (Holy 28), backed by 3 protective wards. Reactive triage — see the wound, close the wound. The safest healer fantasy on the realm.

statPriority.pve: Spell Power > Haste > Critical Strike > Intellect > MP5 > Spirit
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Haste > Critical Strike > Intellect
statPriority.note: Direct/HoT heals (Shine, Sunshine, Solar Beam, Guidance line) scale off Spell Power and carry meaningful cast times, and crit-triggered payoffs (Everglow refresh, Resplendence guaranteed-crit, Lightward) make Haste (more casts per fight) and Crit (bonus procs) both outrank raw Intellect.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Valkyrie
specs.description: Corrected from the full tree: Valkyr's Grip lets you dual-wield TWO-HANDED maces, swords or polearms; Glorious Execution strikes with both and refunds mana, with Odyn's Vanguard resetting it on proc, and Sunslam stunning on holy impact. A val'kyr juggernaut — pure melee DPS, not the tank the class blurb implied.
specs.playstyle: Corrected from the full tree: Valkyr's Grip lets you dual-wield TWO-HANDED maces, swords or polearms; Glorious Execution strikes with both and refunds mana, with Odyn's Vanguard resetting it on proc, and Sunslam stunning on holy impact. A val'kyr juggernaut — pure melee DPS, not the tank the class blurb implied.

statPriority.pve: Hit (to dual-wield special/auto cap, until your dual-wield white swings land) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit (cap) > Expertise (cap) > Strength > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Dual-wielding two two-handers for weapon-damage finishers (Glorious Execution, Justice) is the worst-case miss profile in the game, so Hit then Expertise cap before Strength (Valorous Champion turns Strength into AP + spell-hit) scales the weapon damage. The kit is proc-driven (Odyn's Vanguard-style Glorious Execution resets), so Critical Strike is weighted above Haste and Armor Penetration rather than behind them.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Justice requires a two-hander.

specs.name: Seraphim
specs.description: Take the Valkyron Oath and charge into the front lines of battle to protect your allies from evil, gaining a rage bar and the ability to wield shields and heavy armor.
specs.playstyle: Angelic war-form: the biggest Holy kit in the game (38 Holy-school abilities) with ascension windows, wards and radiant burst. Where Piety smites steadily, Seraphim transforms and erupts.

statPriority.pve: Strength > Spell Hit (until your Holy/Fire attacks stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Attack Power > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Strength > Spell Hit (cap) > Spell Power > Attack Power > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Holy Strength converts Strength into spell power while Solar Might/Impartial raise Strength directly, so a single point of Strength scales BOTH the physical (attack power) and holy/fire sides of every hybrid formula (Justicar's Wrath, Dawnbreak, Hammer of Kings) — making Strength the true primary, not the co-equal Intellect the old note implied. Spell Hit still gates the spell attacks (Justicar's Wrath, Angel's Calling), so it caps ahead of raw Spell Power/Attack Power.

weapon.note: Sword-and-board — Dawnbreak requires a shield.

classes.name: Tinker
classes.summary: Tinkers are genius engineers that use their extensive knowledge of technology for military purposes. Some excel in the use of firearms, while others rely on their mechanical inventions to operate on their behalf.

specs.name: Demolition
specs.description: Explosives and ordnance: bombs, rockets and the class's Ammo System (Rifle/Shotgun/Sniper swaps) driving Fire-and-Physical AoE demolition. Manage ammo, place charges, enjoy the fireworks.
specs.playstyle: Explosives and ordnance: bombs, rockets and the class's Ammo System (Rifle/Shotgun/Sniper swaps) driving Fire-and-Physical AoE demolition. Manage ammo, place charges, enjoy the fireworks.

statPriority.pve: Hit (ranged special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target)) > Critical Strike > Haste > Agility > Attack Power > Spell Power > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit (ranged) > Critical Strike > Agility > Haste > Spell Power
statPriority.note: Core damage rides weapon-based Rounds (Blackpowder Shot, Ranged Weapon Damage plus X) that need Hit to land, while a crit/crit-effectiveness-heavy talent kit (Precision Scopes, Firearm Training, always/guaranteed-crit Air Strike and Take Aim) and cadence talents (Deft Hands, Hotswap, Rounds and Rounds) push Crit and Haste ahead of the Agility/AP base and the secondary RAP*/SP* hybrid bomb scaling.

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Tracer Augmentation requires one.

specs.name: Invention
specs.description: Construct useful inventions or mechs to aid you in combat or provide utility for you and your group.
specs.playstyle: The gadget healer: 5 heal effects delivered through nanobots, deployables and the Clockwork Assistant pet (9 construct effects in the kit). You engineer the healing before it's needed — machines mend the party while you keep the machines running.

statPriority.pve: Spell Power > Intellect > Spirit > Haste > Critical Strike > MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Intellect > Spirit > Haste
statPriority.note: Every heal scales directly off Spell Power (Nanobot Reconstruction's SP, ZIGGI-6K's SP, My Greatest Invention's SP) with explicit 25%-of-Intellect and 25%-of-Spirit healing-power conversions (Battle Medic, Medical Training) layered on top, and Haste accelerates its many periodic ticks (Nanobot Reconstruction, Restorative Beacon) and few cast-time heals (Repair Shot, Hastened Shots).

weapon.note: Ranged weapon — Stim Augmentation requires one.

specs.name: Mechanics
specs.description: Mech-pilot warfare: build and command Bruiser and Strider Bots (11 construct effects) with real defensive plating (6 mitigation effects) — a pet-commander bruiser that can flex toward off-tanking behind its machines.
specs.playstyle: Mech-pilot warfare: build and command Bruiser and Strider Bots (11 construct effects) with real defensive plating (6 mitigation effects) — a pet-commander bruiser that can flex toward off-tanking behind its machines.
