statPriority.pve: Hit (melee special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target)) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Attack Power > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit > Expertise > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Output is flat-Physical and melee-weapon damage (Scrap Slam's 105% Weapon Damage, Bot: Hydraulic Strike, Bot: Crash & Thrash) delivered alongside a commanded pet whose own scaling (Combat Augment: Flame Jets, Sentry Turret's AP) piggybacks on the Tinker's stats, so Hit/Expertise to keep both attacker and pet landing come first, then Agility/AP for the shared melee base, with Crit/Haste feeding extra-attack procs like Improved Spanner Smash.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Venomancer
classes.summary: Venomancers are a collection of druidic peoples devoted to Shadra, the Loa of Spiders. Shadra allows her followers to take the form of a giant Spider or Scorpid, imbuing them with healing and defensive magics.

specs.name: Rot
specs.description: Poison your enemies by invoking Shadra to grant you offensive Nature magics, healing power, and the ability to conjure spider webs.
specs.playstyle: COA's most inverted healer: poison enemies by Shadra's grace and transmute affliction into ally healing (14 DoT effects feeding 5 heal + 3 HoT effects). Your healing throughput starts with your poisons ticking — a fully proactive, damage-woven healer unlike anything on Bronzebeard.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target — poison DoTs must land before any healing is generated) > Haste (Acceleration, Rotpriest, Genesis, and Weaver Form all explicitly speed up periodic ticks, multiplying both damage-to-heal conversion and mana efficiency) > Spell Power > Intellect (mana pool + healing power, per Vital Mending's direct Int-to-healing conversion) > Critical Strike (Worship/Shadra's Command) > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit > Haste > Spell Power > Intellect > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Its healing is entirely downstream of poison DoTs that can miss, and a cluster of talents (Acceleration, Rotpriest, Genesis, Weaver Form) explicitly reward faster ticking, so hit-to-land and haste both outrank raw spell power for this dot-to-heal transmuter.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Stalking
specs.description: Take the form of a giant spider to skitter through the shadows and assassinate foes with fangs, poisons, and impressive speed.
specs.playstyle: Giant-spider assassination: skitter through shadows, ambush, and let 10 poison effects do the murdering after you've moved on. Hit-and-fade melee where the target dies on a delay.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target — the core bites Nerubian Sting, Widowmaker, Facemelter, Withering Venom are Shadow/Nature/Plague school and must land) > Spell Power (direct +SP coefficients on those bites) > Intellect (Prowler converts Intellect into spell damage AND hit rating; Deadly Venom/Precision Bites convert it into crit rating) > Haste (Skitter Smack/Acidfang stack melee haste, speeding rage generation and proc uptime) > Critical Strike (Boding Dread/Glory To Shadra Shadow/Nature spell crit) > Hit/Expertise (keeps auto-attack procs like Drenched Fangs landing) > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Despite the melee/Spider Form delivery, nearly every damaging bite is a Shadow/Nature/Plague spell with explicit +SP scaling, and the Prowler passive (not 'Fang's Guidance', which does not exist in the kit) converts Intellect into both spell damage and hit rating — so this is a spell-hit-and-spell-power spec wearing a rogue's positioning rules.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Fortitude
specs.description: Transform into an armored Scorpid, allowing you to sustain massive amounts of damage and protect your allies from harm.
specs.playstyle: Armored Scorpid tanking: transform and absorb massive punishment behind chitin (5 defensives, 2 HoTs, Nature 38) while 8 venom effects punish attackers. A self-sustaining poison-tank — damage into you becomes damage over them.

statPriority.pve: Agility (the top scaling stat by far — it feeds the attack power behind Chitin Rush / Carapace Crash / Hivebreak / Venomtip, plus critical strike AND dodge, so it triple-dips damage and avoidance; bisbeard weights it ~2.5, above everything else) > Stamina (raw tank HP, and Claw Strike scales off it) > Critical Strike > Haste > Attack Power / Weapon damage > Defense / Dodge rating to the crit-immunity cap > Armor > Intellect (Beetle Form converts a share of Intellect into max health and it feeds the self-heals, but bisbeard weights it only ~0.5 — a distant secondary, not a lead stat)
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Agility > Critical Strike > Defense rating > Dodge rating > Intellect > Armor
statPriority.note: Agility is the master stat despite the tank role: it converts to attack power (your Nature-poison damage), critical strike, and dodge all at once, so both the Moony cheat sheet (Stam > Agi > SP >= AP > Int) and the bisbeard sim weights (Agility ~2.5 vs Intellect ~0.5) put it far above Intellect. Beetle Form's Intellect->max-health and the Int-scaled self-heals are real but a distant second — the earlier 'Intellect leads Agility' read was wrong.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Vizier
specs.description: Confirmed by its full tree as the gentle serpent-healer: Mending Mist blankets up to 8 allies in HoTs, Shadra's Vigil rides on every Serpent's Fang heal, and Mycelial Healing stacks your periodic output ever higher. Where Venom transmutes poison, Vizier simply soothes — COA's purest HoT healer.
specs.playstyle: Confirmed by its full tree as the gentle serpent-healer: Mending Mist blankets up to 8 allies in HoTs, Shadra's Vigil rides on every Serpent's Fang heal, and Mycelial Healing stacks your periodic output ever higher. Where Venom transmutes poison, Vizier simply soothes — COA's purest HoT healer.

statPriority.pve: Haste (Vizier Form explicitly lets its HoT effects benefit from spell haste — faster ticks mean more allies topped off per GCD) > Spell Power > Intellect (mana pool and scales heals like Venomcult Blessing/Serpent Lord's Amulet) > Spirit/MP5 (heavy mana costs — Unity 25%, Shadra's Aid 19%, Ritual Healing 17% of base mana) > Critical Strike > Stamina
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Haste > Spell Power > Intellect > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.note: Every ability targets allies rather than enemies (no spell-hit requirement at all), and Vizier Form calls out spell haste benefiting its HoTs directly, so haste-driven tick throughput plus mana sustain for its very expensive kit outweigh raw spell power or crit.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Reaper
classes.summary: Reapers are warriors who have come upon a dark and hungry spirit trapped inside a scythe, born of souls the Lich King imprisoned within enchanted Hungering Scythes. These 'free' Reapers resist the call of the scourge while wielding the immense power the weapons grant them.

specs.name: Harvest
specs.description: The scythe at work: siphoning two-handed sweeps that harvest the class's Reaped Souls (Essence Harvester is literally in the kit). Build souls with every cut, then trigger Soul Infusion at cap. Grim, rhythmic builder-spender melee. Harvester bakes in 15% lifesteal on ALL damage — the self-sufficiency is the spec.
specs.playstyle: The scythe at work: siphoning two-handed sweeps that harvest the class's Reaped Souls (Essence Harvester is literally in the kit). Build souls with every cut, then trigger Soul Infusion at cap. Grim, rhythmic builder-spender melee. Harvester bakes in 15% lifesteal on ALL damage — the self-sufficiency is the spec.

statPriority.pve: Hit (melee/special, until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit > Expertise > Strength > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Crow's Harvest/Slaughter/Doomrend/Cull are all weapon-damage-% two-hand Physical hits that must land and not be dodged (no offhand to cover a miss), Annihilation explicitly doubles down on Armor Penetration, and Darkrend Scythe's bleed 'scales with haste' while Harvester's flat 15% lifesteal on all damage means Critical Strike compounds the self-heal on every crit.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Crow's Harvest requires a two-hander.

specs.name: Soul
specs.description: Collect the souls of fallen enemies to unleash devastating shadow magics, or skulk through the shadows with dual scytheblades, striking down unsuspecting enemies with ghostly precision.
specs.playstyle: Spend what you reap: collected souls fuel devastating Shadow magic (43 shadow-school abilities — the class's caster payoff line). The Reaper who converts melee harvesting into ranged devastation.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your Shadow spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Agility > Attack Power > Critical Strike > Haste > Intellect
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Hit > Agility > Attack Power > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Dark Pact converts 150% of Agility into Attack Power, and the payoff kit is AP-driven (Deathchaser, Gravesite, Soulrot, Spectre Stride, Jailer's Call, plus the AP terms in Soulrend and Wraithblade) — 7 AP-scaling abilities vs 2 spell-power ones, so Agility is the true primary. Spell Hit still leads because the Shadow-school damage must land; Soul Catcher's Int-to-spell-damage/crit and the small spell power terms make Intellect only a minor tail stat.

weapon.note: One-hand melee — Dirge requires a one-hander (dual-wield or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Domination
specs.description: Weaken enemies and sustain yourself with dark protection magic to defend your allies from harm.
specs.playstyle: Weaken enemies and shield the group with dark protections: a debuff-support line (wards, an enslaved pet effect, enemy-sapping auras) that makes every enemy hit softer and every ally tougher. The death-knight-flavored utility pick.

statPriority.pve: Hit > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Stamina > Strength > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit > Expertise > Strength
statPriority.note: Soulwarden's Resolve, Spectral Warden, and Soulstorm all explicitly scale their armor/magic-resist, ally shield, and enemy-AP-reduction debuff directly off the caster's own Stamina (with Jailer's Bargain/Bolstered Form adding Stamina-adjacent max-health cooldowns), making Stamina the real payoff stat for this support spec behind simply landing its weapon-based Requiem/Dreadwake hits.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Dreadwake requires a two-hander.

classes.name: Primalist
classes.summary: Primalists are an elite group of the Earthen Ring that have refined their primal shamanic and druidic abilities for battle, invoking bestial strength to empower their blows. They are also master shapers of the Earth, with the strongest taking the form of an Earth Elemental to defend their allies.

specs.name: Wildwalker
specs.description: Harness the power of the great beasts of Azeroth to make powerful melee attacks and generate Rage.
specs.playstyle: Channel Azeroth's great beasts: melee strikes stacked with Boons (up to 3 — e.g. Boon of the Bear) and party-buffing Roars (9 buff effects). A shout-heavy bruiser whose presence makes the whole group hit harder. Signature: you can TAME BEASTS of Azeroth as companions — the class's hunter side.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength > Critical Strike > Armor Penetration > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit (to cap) > Strength > Expertise > Critical Strike > Armor Penetration
statPriority.note: Pure attack power physical kit (Wildaxe/Totemic Smash/Primal Shred/Rexxar's Might) whose signature effects are crit-gated (Legacy of Rexxar, Rexxar's Might bleed-on-crit, Druid of the Claw, Bestial Strikes, My Totem My Rules). Because the kit is crit-driven, Critical Strike outranks Armor Penetration; primary attribute is Strength (AP).

weapon.note: Melee — abilities accept any melee weapon; no specific type is required.

specs.name: Geomancy
specs.description: Spend Rage to sunder the Earth and destroy enemies with the destructive forces of the elements.
specs.playstyle: Spend Rage to sunder the earth: seismic Fire-and-Nature slams with serious stone defensives (7 mitigation effects) — an earthshaker that borders on off-tank. Break the ground, wear the mountain.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Intellect > Haste > Critical Strike > Strength > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Spell Power > Spell Hit > Intellect > Haste > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Geomancy's real throughput (Earthspine, Earthquake, Stonefall, Seismic Spike, Eruption) is cast Nature/Fire spellwork carrying Spell Power terms alongside smaller Attack Power terms, and Body of Magma literally converts Intellect into spell haste and spell crit, so caster hit/Spell Power/Intellect rank above the secondary Strength/Armor Pen that only benefit the minor physical components and stone defensives.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Grovekeeper
specs.description: Spend Rage to heal allies with natural creation magic, and grant them short-lasting boons related to different beasts.
specs.playstyle: Healing that runs on Rage: creation magic (4 heals, 3 HoTs on a Nature core) fueled by the class's fury — you fight to fill the bar, then bloom it into the party. A rhythm unlike mana healers: aggression literally becomes restoration.

statPriority.pve: Strength (Attack Power) > Haste > Intellect > Critical Strike > Spirit/MP5
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Strength (Attack Power) > Haste > Spirit/MP5 > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Grovekeeper L15 passive turns Attack Power into spell/healing power and Strength into melee haste + spell hit, and the heals themselves scale off attack power+healing power (Hand of the Earthmother, Spirit Charge, Seismic Wave, Earthmother's Binding, Grove Guardian) rather than spell power. So Strength/Attack Power is the primary throughput stat, not Spell Power; Haste (extra HoT ticks/faster casts) follows. Pure healing, so no spell-hit line in PvE.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Mountain King
specs.description: The dwarf-legend line read from its full tree: Quake stomps on charges, Aftershock procs off auto-attacks, Stone Soldier boosts off-hand damage, and Unyielding Form stacks parry/dodge/armor. A dual-wielding seismic bruiser that off-tanks on demand rather than a dedicated main tank.
specs.playstyle: The dwarf-legend line read from its full tree: Quake stomps on charges, Aftershock procs off auto-attacks, Stone Soldier boosts off-hand damage, and Unyielding Form stacks parry/dodge/armor. A dual-wielding seismic bruiser that off-tanks on demand rather than a dedicated main tank.

statPriority.pve: Hit (to the special-attack and dual-wield white hit caps) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Strength > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit (to cap) > Strength > Expertise > Armor Penetration > Critical Strike
statPriority.note: Mountain King is a one-handed, dual-wield-flavored bruiser (Rock Barrier, Stone Edge, Quake, Gaze of Theradras all require a one-hand melee weapon) whose Quake/Mountain Fury specials and sustained auto-attacks need to land and avoid being dodged/parried to keep its stacking mitigation and rage income flowing, so Hit and Expertise cap ahead of the Strength/Armor Pen/Crit/Haste that scale its weapon damage.

weapon.note: One-hand melee — Bash requires a one-hander (dual-wield or 1H + off-hand).

classes.name: Runemaster
classes.summary: Runemasters are the master arcanists of Azeroth, with powers over all forms of magic. The Runemaster specialises in teleportation, and their immense knowledge of the magical arts makes them effective mage hunters.

specs.name: Glyphic
specs.description: Cast powerful arcane spells to vanquish your foes.
specs.playstyle: Pure arcane artillery driven by the class's Essence Combo system (DB line: Spellslinger — and the deepest talent line in COA at 17 talents). Sequence your runes for combo payoffs; the most theorycraft-dense caster on the realm.

statPriority.pve: Spell Hit (until your spells stop missing a +3-level target) > Spell Power > Intellect > Spirit > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Spell Hit (to cap) > Spell Power > Intellect/Stamina > Spirit > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Chaos Engraving directly converts Intellect (15% dmg) and Spirit (10% dmg, 20% crit) into damage/crit while Glyphic Ruin/Thaumaturgy/Primordial Blast/Runic Obliteration scale off Spell Power, so the combo-reliant rotation hit-caps first, then stacks SP/Int/Spirit, with Eye of the Beholder's 25% crit window outweighing the smaller Ancient Teachings/Runeslinger haste procs.

weapon.note: Caster — no weapon-damage abilities in the kit; itemize spell power (a staff or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Engravement
specs.description: Inscribe magical runes to empower your spell casting.
specs.playstyle: Inscribe runes to empower your casting (DB line: Conjuration): a setup-heavy line where damage flows from prepared inscriptions and familiar synergy. Slower cadence, bigger empowered windows.

statPriority.pve: Hit (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit/Expertise (cap) > Agility > Spell Penetration > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Primordial Strength converts attack power into spell damage and Agility into hit/spell-hit, so this is an Agility/AP weapon spec, not a caster. The signature Fists of Power and the whole damage kit (Fist of the Ancients attack power, Runeblade attack power, Runic Brand attack power, all Weapon Engravings) scale off attack power from Agility; only tiny secondary Word of Flames/Oblivion Intellect coefficients exist. Melee hit-then-expertise caps come first, then Agility for AP/crit, with the proc-heavy Weapon Engraving triggers (Engraved and Ready boosts their crit) favoring Crit over Haste. Crucially, the damage is largely ELEMENTAL/magic: every Weapon Engraving procs elemental damage off your hits and Primordial Strength converts attack power into spell damage — so Armor Penetration does nothing here (it only bypasses physical armor). Dual-wield two FAST one-handers to maximize engraving proc frequency (Fists of Power also strikes with your offhand), and value Spell (elemental) Penetration — especially in PvP — to push the magic damage through resistances. Agility + spell penetration is hard to itemize together; spell-pen PvP gems help.

weapon.note: One-hand melee — Fist of the Ancients requires a one-hander (dual-wield or 1H + off-hand).

specs.name: Riftblade
specs.description: Use your knowledge of the Arcane to hunt mages using dual daggers and stealth.
specs.playstyle: The mage-hunter: dual daggers riding arcane rifts, with a striking 8 defensive effects — built to close on casters, cut through their spells and survive the answer. Reactive, anti-magic melee with off-tank bones.

statPriority.pve: Hit (melee special-attack cap (until your specials stop missing a +3-level target), also covers Blade-spell hits) > Expertise (until your attacks stop being dodged/parried) > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste > Armor Penetration
statPriority.pvp: Resilience > Stamina > Hit/Expertise > Agility > Critical Strike > Haste
statPriority.note: Elemental Assassin converts Agility straight into spell damage, spell crit, and hit rating on top of its normal dagger AP/crit contribution, and the reactive proc kit (Primordial Echoes, Surging Slash, Windsage) all trigger off critical strikes, so after hit/expertise caps Agility and Crit outrank the smaller Haste/Armor Pen gains from weapon-damage finishers like Runeblade, Smolder, and Fracture.

weapon.note: Two-handed — Fracture requires a two-hander.
